old_claim.note: Notarial Records provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because craft quality marks are certification and inspection marks, while the source-backed notarial_records node now covers twelfth-century Genoese notarial registers.

source_shape.source_locator: The State Archive of Genoa source supports Giovanni Scriba's notarial register and medieval Genoese notarial archival series; it does not support craft marks as dependent on notarial registers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors notarial registers, not guild marks, hallmarks, or inspection systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping the edge would launder generic legal recordkeeping into a craft-certification node without source support.

ontology_before: Craft quality marks depended directly on notarial_records through a generic expert-inference edge.
ontology_after: Craft quality marks rely on guild and measurement infrastructure only; notarial registers remain a separate legal-document tradition.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: craft_quality_marks no longer has a direct notarial_records prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The craft node were narrowed to notarized certification records rather than marks, hallmarks, seals, and inspections.
A source showed medieval craft quality marks required notarial registers.
A separate notarial certification node were introduced for notarized inspection acts.
short_reason: The reviewed notarial-register sources do not support a direct dependency for craft quality marks.
